lifecycle.operator_decision_summary: Operator 2026-07-02 ordered per-MT drift spot-checks with adversarial sub-agent review; no MT has been validated by an official validator. Wave-B audit: plain TYPING in the code editor records no undo entries - only format and line-op snapshots reach push_code_edit_undo; TextBuffer deliberately has no undo; live Ctrl+Z after typing does nothing in the code pane. MT-079 proof SEEDED the undo entry via push_code_edit_undo rather than recording from a typed edit, masking the gap; the two E11 #[ignore] carries still have unreachable!() bodies at tree tip. Undo-count indicator (AC-6) rendered only by the test bin; artifact PNG is a test-render crop not a live-shell capture. Fix: record typing-edit undo at the bus boundary (batched like RichUndoBatcher), convert both ignored tests to live proofs, mount the indicator or drop AC-6 from live claims. Rich-editor half is genuinely excellent and stays.

scope.summary:
Goal: define and enforce a unified undo scope policy across all editor panes (code, rich-text, graph, canvas), integrating the undo stack with the InteractionBus (MT-031). Handshake's rule: Ctrl+Z always undoes the most recent action in the focused pane; cross-pane actions (e.g. route-to-stage, embed-from-atelier) are recorded as atomic operations in the cross-pane undo stack. Expected diff: (a) a new UndoStack type in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/undo_stack.rs that holds undo/redo rings per pane and a cross-pane ring; (b) each pane registers its local undo/redo with the bus; (c) the bus dispatches Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Y to the focused pane's local undo first, falling back to the cross-pane ring; (d) unit tests proving undo/redo round-trips within and across panes.

The key policy decisions this MT must encode:
  POLICY-1 (local-first): Ctrl+Z in a focused pane undoes the most recent action IN THAT PANE, not the most recent action globally. This matches VS Code and Obsidian behavior.
  POLICY-2 (cross-pane ring): actions that touch multiple panes atomically (embed-from-atelier in MT-033, route-to-stage in MT-033, code-ref insertion in MT-034) are pushed to a cross-pane undo ring. A dedicated 'Undo Cross-Pane' command (Ctrl+Shift+Z) undoes the most recent cross-pane action.
  POLICY-3 (persistence scope): undo history is session-scoped only; it is NOT persisted to the backend. On app restart, undo history is empty. Document this explicitly in code comments.
  POLICY-4 (canvas undo): canvas node moves/creations that call the backend immediately (PUT /canvas/{id}/graph) are NOT in-memory undoable via this stack alone — they require a compensating backend call (re-PUT the previous graph). The cross-pane ring records the compensating call as the undo action.
  POLICY-5 (undo stack cap): local pane undo rings are capped at 200 operations; cross-pane ring is capped at 50 operations. Exceeding the cap silently drops the oldest entry.

Files to CREATE:
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/undo_stack.rs  — defines:
      UndoAction { description: String, undo_fn: Arc<dyn Fn() -> UndoResult + Send + Sync>, redo_fn: Arc<dyn Fn() -> UndoResult + Send + Sync> }
      UndoResult { ok: bool, error: Option<String> }
      PaneUndoRing { pane_id: PaneId, ring: VecDeque<UndoAction>, redo_ring: VecDeque<UndoAction>, cap: usize }
      CrossPaneUndoRing { ring: VecDeque<UndoAction>, redo_ring: VecDeque<UndoAction>, cap: usize }
      UnifiedUndoScope { pane_rings: HashMap<PaneId, PaneUndoRing>, cross_pane_ring: CrossPaneUndoRing }
      Methods: push_local(pane_id, action), undo_local(pane_id) -> Option<UndoResult>, redo_local(pane_id) -> Option<UndoResult>, push_cross_pane(action), undo_cross_pane() -> Option<UndoResult>, redo_cross_pane() -> Option<UndoResult>.
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_undo_scope.rs  — unit and integration tests.

Files to EDIT:
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/lib.rs  — add `pub mod undo_stack;`
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/interaction_bus.rs  — add UnifiedUndoScope as a field of InteractionBus. Reuse the existing WP-011 command bus (command_registry.rs) and event bus (event_bus.rs) for command registration and dispatch. Add methods: bus.push_undo_local(pane_id, action), bus.undo(pane_id) (dispatches to local ring first), bus.undo_cross_pane(), bus.redo(pane_id). Register 'undo' command (Ctrl+Z) and 'redo' command (Ctrl+Y) via command_registry.rs. Register 'undo-cross-pane' command (Ctrl+Shift+Z) via command_registry.rs.
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/undo.rs  — replace any existing per-pane undo state with bus.push_undo_local calls. Each text edit (character insert, delete, paste) pushes a UndoAction where undo_fn restores the previous rope state.
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/undo.rs  — same: each block mutation (insert node, delete node, update attrs) pushes to bus.push_undo_local. The undo_fn restores the previous document tree snapshot.
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_graph.rs  — (REUSE existing WP-011 graph module) node moves and tag changes push to bus.push_undo_local.
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/canvas_board.rs  — (REUSE existing WP-011 canvas module) canvas graph mutations push to bus.push_cross_pane (because they call the backend); undo_fn re-PUTs the previous canvas graph.
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/stage_pane.rs  — route-to-stage pushes to bus.push_cross_pane; undo_fn reverts StageContent to previous value.

Reuse pointers (existing WP-011 modules — do not recreate):
  - command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs provide the shared command/selection/event bus that the InteractionBus builds on; the unified undo commands are registered through command_registry.rs and dispatched via event_bus.rs.
  - loom_graph.rs is the existing graph module; the graph-pane local undo wires into it.
  - canvas_board.rs is the existing canvas module; the canvas cross-pane undo wires into it.
  - command_palette.rs + command_registry.rs back the 'Show Undo History' command palette entry.
  - top_menu_bar.rs backs any menu surfacing of undo/redo.
  - accessibility/* provides the AccessKit author_id/role used for the undo-count indicator.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. UndoAction closures capture the minimum state needed. For code/rich-text local undo: capture a clone of the rope/doc tree snapshot BEFORE the edit. undo_fn replaces current rope/doc with snapshot; redo_fn re-applies the edit.
  2. Rope snapshot cloning (ropey::Rope) is O(1) due to ropey's arc-based persistent data structure — safe to clone per edit.
  3. Rich-text doc snapshot: clone the JSONContent tree (serde_json::Value). For large documents this is O(n); apply a debounce: group edits within the same 500ms window into a single undo entry to avoid O(n) per keystroke.
  4. Canvas undo: the undo_fn for a canvas node creation calls PUT /canvas/{canvas_id}/graph with the previous nodes list (minus the created node). This is a compensating backend call. It must be async; in the UndoAction, undo_fn should be an async fn wrapped in Arc<dyn Fn() -> BoxFuture<UndoResult>>. Alternatively, use a channel to dispatch the compensating call to the async runtime.
  5. Keybind dispatch: in bus.dispatch_keybind, match Ctrl+Z -> undo(focused_pane_id), Ctrl+Y -> redo(focused_pane_id), Ctrl+Shift+Z -> undo_cross_pane(). Each call returns Option<UndoResult>; if Some(result) and !result.ok, log the error to the Flight Recorder (MT-036).
  6. Undo menu indicator: in the code pane and rich-text pane title bar, show a small 'Undo ({n})' indicator using the count of items in the local ring. AccessKit: author_id="undo-count-{pane_id}", role=StaticText.
  7. Tests: (a) unit: push 3 actions to a PaneUndoRing, undo 3 times, assert all undo_fns called in reverse order; (b) unit: redo after undo restores state; (c) unit: pushing beyond cap=5 drops the oldest; (d) unit: cross-pane ring undo calls undo_fn of last cross-pane action; (e) integration (real PG): create rich doc, push an undo action that saves a previous version, call undo, assert the doc reverts (use saveRichDocument to a previous content_json); (f) kittest: simulate Ctrl+Z, assert bus.undo called for focused pane.

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
  - MT-031 (InteractionBus) must be merged; UnifiedUndoScope is embedded in InteractionBus.
  - ropey crate must already be in Cargo.toml (from MT-001 code editor).
  - Canvas undo requires async capability in UndoAction closures; ensure the app's async runtime (tokio) is accessible from within the undo_fn.

Risk if missed: without a unified undo policy, each pane implements its own ad-hoc undo (or none), users experience unexpected behavior (Ctrl+Z in one pane undoes another, or cross-pane edits are not undoable at all), and the parity proof suite cannot assert 'one unified undo scope' as required by the interconnection contract.
acceptance_criteria: POLICY-1 (local-first): Ctrl+Z in the code pane undoes the last code edit and does NOT affect the rich-text pane's document state — proven by kittest: make edit in code pane, make edit in rich-text pane, focus code pane, press Ctrl+Z, assert only code pane reverted.

POLICY-2 (cross-pane ring): after an embed-from-atelier action (MT-033) or route-to-stage action, pressing Ctrl+Shift+Z undoes the cross-pane action — proven by kittest: trigger cross-pane action, press Ctrl+Shift+Z, assert action reverted.

POLICY-3 (persistence scope): on app restart, undo history is empty — proven by unit test: create UndoStack, serialize/deserialize, assert all rings are empty (the type must NOT implement Serialize to prevent accidental persistence).

POLICY-4 (canvas undo): undoing a canvas node creation via Ctrl+Shift+Z calls the compensating PUT /canvas/{id}/graph and the node disappears from the next canvas reload — proven by integration test against real PG.
POLICY-5 (cap): pushing 201 actions to a capped-at-200 ring results in 200 entries, not 201 — proven by unit test.
Undo count indicator shows correct count in pane title bar after pushes and undos — proven by kittest: push 3 actions, assert count=3, undo once, assert count=2.
cargo test -p handshake-native undo_scope -- --nocapture passes with no failures.
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake-native undo_scope -- --nocapture  =>  all PASS
kittest: make edit in code pane, make edit in rich-text pane, Ctrl+Z in code pane, assert only code pane reverted
kittest: trigger cross-pane embed action, Ctrl+Shift+Z, assert cross-pane action reverted
integration test (real PG): canvas node creation, Ctrl+Shift+Z undo, reload canvas, assert node absent
unit test: push 201 to cap-200 ring, assert len==200
implementation_notes: ropey::Rope clones are O(1) due to arc-based persistent structure — safe to clone for every code edit undo snapshot. Rich-text JSONContent clones are O(n) — apply the 500ms batching window.

For canvas async undo: define UndoAction.undo_async_fn: Option<Arc<dyn Fn() -> Pin<Box<dyn Future<Output=UndoResult> + Send>> + Send + Sync>>. Local pane undos use the sync undo_fn; canvas undo uses undo_async_fn. The bus dispatches sync and async variants separately.

The React codebase has no shared undo architecture to port from (each surface used its own). This MT is a greenfield design. The VS Code undo model (per-editor undo ring, no cross-editor) and Obsidian's per-note undo model are the behavioral references.
Do NOT attempt to implement CRDT-based undo here; CRDT is handled by the CRDT backend (MT-038/MT-039). This undo stack is purely the in-memory UI-layer undo for immediate local feedback.

Cross-WP dependency: the InteractionBus this MT extends is built atop the WP-011 command bus (command_registry.rs) and event bus (event_bus.rs) shipped in WP-KERNEL-011; reuse those modules rather than introducing a new selection/clipboard/command bus.

PATHS + INTEGRATE-NOT-DUPLICATE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-23): the bus is interop/interaction_bus.rs (MT-031, NOT a root interaction_bus.rs). rich_text/undo.rs -> rich_editor/. canvas_board.rs -> graph/canvas_board.rs (MT-026). CHECK FIRST for EXISTING undo: the code editor (MT-001 ropey buffer + MT-010 keymap) and the rich editor (MT-011..020) may already have local undo/redo (a Ctrl+Z handler). INTEGRATE existing undo into bus.push_undo_local - do NOT add a SECOND parallel undo stack alongside a working one (the wrap-not-fork discipline from MT-031). If a pane has no undo yet, add it via the bus. Code edit state lives on CodeEditorPanel (code_editor/panel.rs).

POLICY-3 NO Serialize + Weak REFS + egui Ctrl+Z SUPPRESSION (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): (POLICY-3/AC-3) UnifiedUndoScope/UndoAction/rings MUST NOT derive or implement Serialize/Deserialize - session-scoped only, never persisted; the AC asserts the type cannot serialize (a #[derive(Serialize)] is a FAILURE). UndoAction closures use Weak<Mutex<PaneState>> back-refs (upgrade only during invocation, skip if dropped) to avoid retain cycles with pane state the bus holds (RISK-3/MC-3). Ctrl+Z: the code + rich editors are CUSTOM widgets (ropey buffer / rich_editor_widget, NOT egui::TextEdit) so the bus undo is the path; for ANY egui::TextEdit field call ui.input_mut(|i| i.consume_shortcut(&ctrl_z)) BEFORE delegating so egui's built-in text undo does not also fire (RISK-4/MC-4, the MT-031 keybind-suppression pattern). Ctrl+Shift+Z = cross-pane; Ctrl+Y = redo - via command_registry.

CANVAS COMPENSATING UNDO via MT-026 ROUTES + REFACTOR SAFETY (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): (POLICY-4) the canvas node create/move undo is a COMPENSATING backend call - align it with MT-026's VERIFIED /loom/canvas/{block_id}/placements routes (the CanvasBoardClient delete/move), NOT the contract's PUT /canvas/{id}/graph (the MT-033 correction). Snapshot the previous placement AT ACTION-CREATE time, not at undo-invoke time (RISK-2/MC-2 - else intermediate edits get clobbered); async undo_fn via the existing tokio bridge. This MT touches 5 pane modules (code/rich/graph/canvas/stage) - the FULL suite (every bin: the correctness lens runs bare cargo test) MUST stay green; the undo-ring data structure (push_local/undo/redo/cap=200, cross-pane cap=50, local-first, no-Serialize) is PURE + unit-testable STANDALONE; rich-text 500ms batching window (RISK-1/MC-1); canvas-compensating + rich-doc-revert are REAL-PG -> NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF. NO Color32 literal (undo-count indicator uses a palette token); accesskit undo-count-{pane_id}(StaticText). NOT CRDT undo (that is MT-038/039) - this is in-memory UI undo only.

HARDEN RESOLUTION (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-23, after the 3-lens panel caught UNWIRED SCAFFOLDING + the harden stage died on a transient API 500): the impl shipped a REAL, tested undo data structure (undo_stack.rs) + bus methods + per-pane adapters, full suite green - BUT every push_*/register_undo_commands/set_undo_runtime/RichUndoBatcher entrypoint is reachable ONLY from the test (zero live call sites). RESOLUTION (deliverable-honest, no scaffolding-hiding): (1) RICH PANE - FIX NOW: the rich editor widget has a LIVE input loop (rich_editor/renderer/input_handler.rs:~223) that uses its OWN parallel UndoManager - the exact second undo stack the contract forbade. WIRE the rich widget's block-mutation + Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Y through bus.push_rich_edit_undo (gated by a real RichUndoBatcher 500ms instance) + bus.undo(pane)/redo(pane), REMOVE/retire the parallel UndoManager, and re-prove AC-1's rich half through the REAL rich-widget kittest path (drive a real edit + keystroke), NOT the sync_action logging stand-in. (2) CODE PANE + CANVAS + app.rs RUNTIME INSTALL + register_undo_commands - BLOCKED_ON_DEPENDENCY E11/MT-069: these require the code/canvas editor panes MOUNTED in app.rs (the host-mount work E11 owns). Do NOT fake a live wire into an unmounted pane. Reclassify AC-1's code half + AC-2's canvas half + AC-6 live-count as BLOCKED_ON_DEPENDENCY (carried to MT-069); keep the data-structure + adapter ACs (POLICY-3/4-shape/5) PASS. No dead code may be asserted as a PASS via a stand-in.
ports_from_react: No direct React parity source — the React version has no shared undo implementation. VS Code undo model (per-editor ring) and Obsidian per-note undo are behavioral references.
binds_backend_api: PUT /canvas/{canvas_id}/graph  =>  api.ts updateCanvasGraph  =>  used in canvas undo compensating call to restore previous graph state
POST /knowledge/documents/{doc_id}  =>  api.ts saveRichDocument  =>  used in rich-text undo compensating call to restore previous content_json (only for cross-pane undo; local undo is in-memory only)

risks: Rich-text doc snapshot per keystroke at O(n) causes lag on large documents if the 500ms batching window is not implemented or if serde_json cloning is unexpectedly slow.
Canvas async undo_fn holding a stale copy of the previous canvas graph: if the user makes further canvas edits before the undo fires, the compensating PUT may clobber the intermediate edits.
UndoAction closures capturing Arc references to pane state create retain cycles if pane state is also held by the InteractionBus; must use Weak references for back-references.
Ctrl+Z keybind conflict with text-widget default undo in egui: if egui processes Ctrl+Z for its own text fields before the bus, the bus undo never fires in text-input contexts.
Cross-pane undo ring may interleave with local pane undo if the user alternates pane actions rapidly, confusing the undo order visible to the user.
minimum_controls: Implement and unit-test the 500ms batching window for rich-text undo; assert that rapid keystrokes within 500ms produce one undo entry, not N.
Canvas async undo: record the graph snapshot at the time of the action creation, not at the time of undo invocation. Store the snapshot in the UndoAction closure.
Use Weak<Mutex<PaneState>> in UndoAction closures; upgrade to Arc only during undo invocation; skip silently if the pane was dropped.
In bus.dispatch_keybind for Ctrl+Z: call ui.input_mut(|i| i.consume_shortcut(&ctrl_z)) before delegating to undo, so egui's text-widget handler does not also fire.
Expose a visual undo history inspector (accessible via command palette: 'Show Undo History') showing the last 10 local + cross-pane entries; this makes the policy testable by humans and swarm agents.

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate + MT-031 bus/keybind-suppression + MT-010 keymap undo + MT-026 canvas routes + MT-033 canvas-route correction
corrections: interop/interaction_bus.rs + rich_editor/ + graph/canvas_board.rs paths; integrate existing code/rich undo, don't duplicate
POLICY-3 no Serialize (session-scoped); Weak back-refs (no retain cycle); egui Ctrl+Z consume_shortcut suppression
canvas compensating undo via MT-026 /loom/canvas placement routes (not PUT /canvas/graph); snapshot at action-create time
undo-ring pure+standalone (cap/local-first/cross-pane); 5-pane touch -> full suite green; real-PG compensating NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF; no Color32; not CRDT

kernel_builder_harden_resolution.root_cause: unwired scaffolding (MT-008/010/014 class) - undo entrypoints reachable only from the test; rich pane kept a parallel UndoManager; harden died on transient API 500 leaving partial edits (discarded).
kernel_builder_harden_resolution.fix_now: wire rich-pane live undo through the bus + RichUndoBatcher; remove parallel UndoManager; re-prove AC-1 rich through the real rich-widget path.
kernel_builder_harden_resolution.deferred_to_MT_069: code-pane + canvas live undo wiring + app.rs set_undo_runtime + register_undo_commands (need the editor panes mounted - E11 host-mount).

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_af4edb47 (impl + 3-lens panel) + re-harden agent a7ef9daa (the pipeline harden died on a transient API 500; re-run honestly)
commits: ea0d53ea feat MT-035 unified undo scope (data structure + bus + adapters)
026691f8 fix harden - wire rich-pane live undo through bus; defer code/canvas live wiring to E11
implementation_result.review_panel: correctness=CHANGES_REQUIRED(1), backend-shape=CHANGES_REQUIRED(2), perf-focus-hygiene=PASS(0) -> caught UNWIRED SCAFFOLDING (MT-008/010/014 class): undo entrypoints reachable only from the test; rich pane kept a parallel UndoManager; AC-1 rich-half proven via a fake logging action.
implementation_result.harden_500_recovery: The automated harden stage died on a transient API 500 leaving partial uncommitted edits in 6 files; KERNEL_BUILDER discarded them (clean tree -> ea0d53ea) and re-ran a focused coder-protocol harden agent with a precise rich-now/code-defer split.
implementation_result.rich_pane_LIVE: FIXED + INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED: the rich editor widget's live Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Y is now routed through the unified InteractionBus (decode_undo_chord strips the chords before the formatting pass so the parallel UndoManager is no longer consulted; recording via content_json before/after diff incl IME-commit; chord invocation deferred outside the lock to avoid deadlock; pane_id installed live by RichEditorPaneFactory). AC-1 rich-half re-proven by rich_pane_ctrl_z_reverts_through_bus (REAL typed edit + REAL Ctrl+Z keystroke -> bus.local_undo_count==1 then doc reverts via the unified scope). The fake sync_action('rich-edit') stand-in DELETED. RichUndoBatcher 500ms coalescing real-tested (replace_tail coalesces a burst into ONE entry that reverts the whole burst, not gate-only-drop).
implementation_result.code_canvas_DEFERRED_E11: HONEST typed dependency (NOT faked): code-pane + canvas live undo wiring + app.rs set_undo_runtime + register_undo_commands require the editor panes MOUNTED in app.rs (E11/MT-069). Tests code_pane_live_undo_blocked_on_e11 + canvas_pane_live_placement_undo_blocked_on_e11 are #[ignore]d with the E11 dependency named; the push_code_edit_undo/push_canvas_placement_undo adapters stay as tested data-structure helpers (relabeled, NOT asserted as live). Carry-forward recorded to MT-069.
implementation_result.proofs: POLICY-3 (no-Serialize), POLICY-4 (canvas compensating DELETE via verified MT-026 /loom/canvas-placements route SHAPE), POLICY-5 (caps 200/50) PASS; rich-pane LIVE proven; code/canvas live = BLOCKED_ON_DEPENDENCY E11. INDEPENDENT full-suite verify: exit 0, 86 bins, every bin 0 failed (1724 passed/0 failed/16 ignored); test_theme 19 incl no_hardcoded_color32 (CONTROL-4 green); clippy --all-targets clean; git ls-files src/**/*.png empty.
implementation_result.waiver_granted: undo_stack.rs + interop/interaction_bus.rs + rich_editor/mod.rs + rich_editor/renderer/rich_editor_widget.rs + tests/test_undo_scope.rs in allowed_paths.
